title: Implement Queue using Stacks
description:
Implement a first-in-first-out (FIFO) queue using only two stacks.

Given arrays `operations` (strings: `"push"`, `"pop"`, `"peek"`, `"empty"`) and `values` (corresponding values — `null` for non-push ops), execute them on a queue implemented with two stacks and return an array of results:
- `push(x)` → `null`
- `pop()` → the removed front value
- `peek()` → the front value without removing it
- `empty()` → `true` or `false`
hints: Use two stacks: one for input, one for output.
Transfer elements from input to output only when output is empty.
This lazy transfer gives amortized O(1) per operation.
